articles.title: USD/CAD trims losses as geopolitics overshadow US CPI and Canadian jobs data
articles.expand: USD/CAD reverses earlier losses on Friday, as uncertainty surrounding upcoming US-Iran negotiations keeps markets cautious and overshadows US inflation and Canadian employment data, keeping price action volatile.

articles.title: AUD/USD muted as hot US CPI reinforces Fed’s higher-for-longer stance
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair steadies on Friday, posting a slight decline after a four-day strike of gains, trading with a cautious tone amid fresh data releases in the US and geopolitical risks.

articles.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: Technicals turn slightly bullish for Pound Sterling amid Mideast drama
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) staged a stellar recovery from near four-month lows against the US Dollar (USD) and clinched two-month highs just shy of the 1.3500 threshold.

articles.title: CNN. Trump had a "tense" call with Netanyahu on Lebanon
articles.expand: The geopolitical tension continues with reports that Trump had a tense call with Israel Netanyahu on Lebanon. Trump wants Israel to slow down its bombings on Lebanon while negotiations for a cease-fire continue with Iran. Netanyahu does not want to stop the bombing. In other geopolitical news: Iran also wants the release of blocked assets prior to ...

articles.title: JPY: Neutral rate debate supports gradual gains – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur expects the Bank of Japan (BoJ) to deliver two further rate hikes in 2026, taking policy closer to a rising neutral rate and supporting a modest Japanese Yen appreciation versus Dollar (USD) and Euro (EUR) in the second half of the year.

articles.title: USD/CAD forms a gigantic range after CA Employment – Will lower Oil prices endanger the CAD?
articles.expand: USD/CAD and EUR/CAD update: Canadian employment data delivers a steady +14.1K jobs and a lower 6.7% unemployment rate, but a hot 4.7% year-over-year wage growth keeps hawkish Bank of Canada bets alive. Meanwhile, the Loonie navigates a shifting global landscape as a sudden US-Iran ceasefire triggers a 15% plunge in WTI prices. Explore a two-timefra...

articles.title: EUR/USD extends gains as ceasefire optimism weighs on USD despite firm CPI
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) edges higher against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday, with EUR/USD extending gains for a fifth straight day, as improving risk sentiment following the US-Iran ceasefire announcement offsets the impact of firm US inflation data and keeps the Greenback under pressure.

articles.title: US: Energy shock complicates Fed path – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Bernd Weidensteiner notes that U.S. inflation jumped to 3.3% in March, driven mainly by higher gasoline prices linked to the war in Iran, while core inflation remains moderate.

articles.title: White House Adviser Hassett: We expect rapid reduction in energy prices once Strait of Hormuz opens
articles.expand: Kevin Hassett, Director of the National Economic Council, told FOX Business on Friday that the Federal Reserve (Fed) outlook for having room to cut rates will be very solid. He also claimed the Hormuz Strait can be reopened within two months, saying they have backup plans to reopen it.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP rises on Russia-Ukraine peace optimism
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) trades higher against its major currency peers, except other European currencies, edging up to near 1.3444 against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Friday.

articles.title: NZD: Hawkish pricing seen as demanding – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note New Zealand Dollar (NZD) has rallied on hawkish Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) rhetoric and easing Oil risks, but warns markets may be overpricing tightening.

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Bulls eye breakout above 214.00-215.00 resistance
articles.expand: GBP/JPY edges higher on Friday, extending gains for a fifth straight day as the Japanese Yen (JPY) remains on the defensive against most of the major peers. Elevated Oil prices continue to weigh on the Yen, given Japan’s status as a major net importer.

articles.title: Breaking News: US inflation surges to 2-year high of 3.3%, Dollar Index (DXY) slips as US-Iran talks in focus
articles.expand: US inflation hits a 2-year high of 3.3% due to a massive energy "war premium" from geopolitical conflict. Core CPI remains contained at 2.6%, creating a "sticky" situation for the Fed. Markets watch US-Iran talks, which are steering the US Dollar Index (DXY).

articles.title: US CPI at 3.3% Misses Expectations, Second-Round Effects Still Limited
articles.expand: US inflation surged in March, with CPI rising 0.9% mom and lifting the annual rate from 2.4% yoy to 3.3% yoy. While the jump was significant, both figures came in slightly below expectations of 1.0% mom and 3.4% yoy respectively, suggesting the energy-driven spike was largely anticipated. The surge was overwhelmingly driven by energy costs. [&#8230...

articles.title: Breaking: US CPI inflation jumps to 3.3% in March, as forecast
articles.expand: Annual inflation in the United States (US), as measured by the change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI), climbed to 3.3% in March, the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported on Friday. This print followed 2.4% in February and came in line with market expectations.

articles.title: NBP expands gold reserves and moves closer to the global Top 10
articles.expand: The NBP increased its gold reserves in March 2026 by about 13 tons, taking advantage of a sharp price correction. Total holdings rose to around 580 tons. Despite a lower valuation due to falling prices, the central bank continues its strategy of accumulation and is now just over 30 tons away from entering the global top 10 in gold reserves.

articles.expand: The markets are treading water as the North American session gets underway, with little conviction across the major asset classes ahead of key economic releases. articles.title: USD/CAD Forecast: Bears turn cautious ahead of US-Iran talks, US CPI, Canadian jobs data
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair gains some positive traction on Friday, snapping a four-day losing streak to the 1.3800 neighborhood or over a two-week low set the previous day. Doubts over the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire support the safe-haven US Dollar (USD) and act as a tailwind for spot prices.

articles.expand: AUD/CAD is back on the front foot this week, with its uptrend reasserting itself as markets price in a widening gap between a hawkish RBA and a defensive BoC. articles.title: WTI Crude Oil (USOIL) Analysis for April 10, 2026: Long-Term & Short-Term Correction Levels Amid War Risks
articles.expand: With the two-week US-Iran ceasefire under strain, crude oil sits at a technical crossroads as traders are holding out for further developments to gauge if a brief dip or a longer-term pullback is due.

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Softens to near 0.7900 with rangebound tone ahead of US CPI release
articles.expand: The USD/CHF pair loses ground to near 0.7905 during the early European session on Friday. A fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States (US) and Iran provides some support to a safe-haven currency such as the Swiss Franc (CHF) against the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Why the oil crisis is about to worsen amid the ceasefire that wasn't
articles.expand: The official pledges of the last few days may have placated markets at first, but they have become little more than bargaining chips ahead of this weekend's negotiations between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Global Rate Path Veers Higher in Wake of Another Trump Shock
articles.expand: Donald Trump’s second big shock to the world economy since he returned to the White House looks increasingly likely to force central banks into a new round of tightening.

articles.title: Bendigo Bank signals third interest rate hike for 2026 won’t be until August
articles.expand: A bank has predicted a third interest rate hike in 2026, but has gone against the major banks on when it will hit Australians.

articles.expand: Japan’s wholesale inflation surge highlights rising cost pressures, with the BoJ warning of potential stagflation risks amid the Iran-driven energy shock. articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex EUR/USD rose to 1.1667 on Thursday. The US dollar partially recouped its losses from the previous session, as market sentiment remains cautious amid a fragile truce between the US and Iran. articles.title: The Petrodollar trade is over, Dollar tumbles – EUR/USD, AUD/USD & Dollar Index (DXY) overview
articles.expand: US Dollar, AUD/USD and EUR/USD Analysis: The Greenback’s two-month Petrodollar rally takes a massive hit as an unexpected US-Iran ceasefire agreement shatters war-driven demand. Yesterday's truce headline sent the Dollar Index tumbling. Explore a technical analysis of the Dollar Index, EUR/USD, and AUD/USD to assess the market's decisive turn.
